---
title: "Get involved"
description: "How to start contributing to EpiAware."
---

EpiAware is early and we are actively looking for collaborators. If you care
about composable modelling, infectious disease epidemiology, or building a Julia
ecosystem, there is a place for you here.

## Ways in

- **Discuss.** Join the [epinowcast forum](https://community.epinowcast.org/) to
  ask questions and talk through ideas — the best first step.
- **Try the packages.** Use them on a real problem and tell us what worked and
  what didn't, via the [forum](https://community.epinowcast.org/) or a
  package's GitHub issues.
- **Contribute.** Fix a bug, improve documentation, or add a feature. See the
  [Contributing](contributing.qmd) guide.
- **Bring a use case.** If you have a modelling problem that composable tools
  could help with, open an issue or get in touch — it helps us prioritise.

## Getting in touch

We are still working out how best to onboard new contributors, so for now the
simplest route is to email or post on the forum, and we will point you in the
right direction. Contact
[Sam Abbott](https://www.lshtm.ac.uk/aboutus/people/abbott.sam) at LSHTM, or
open an issue on the [relevant repository](https://github.com/EpiAware).

If you would like to build a substantial piece of the ecosystem, take a look at
the [project proposals](https://github.com/EpiAware/ProjectProposals) for ideas.
